verses.2: “Truly I know that it is so,
verses.3: If he is pleased to contend with him,
verses.4: God is wise in heart, and mighty in strength.
verses.5: He removes the mountains, and they don’t know it,
verses.6: He shakes the earth out of its place.
verses.7: He commands the sun and it doesn’t rise,
verses.8: He alone stretches out the heavens,
verses.9: He makes the Bear, Orion, and the Pleiades,
verses.10: He does great things past finding out;
verses.11: Behold, he goes by me, and I don’t see him.
verses.14: How much less will I answer him,
verses.15: Though I were righteous, yet I wouldn’t answer him.
verses.16: If I had called, and he had answered me,
verses.17: For he breaks me with a storm,
verses.18: He will not allow me to catch my breath,
verses.19: If it is a matter of strength, behold, he is mighty!
verses.20: Though I am righteous, my own mouth will condemn me.
verses.24: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked.
verses.25: “Now my days are swifter than a runner.
verses.26: They have passed away as the swift ships,
verses.27: If I say, ‘I will forget my complaint,
verses.28: I am afraid of all my sorrows.
verses.30: If I wash myself with snow,
verses.31: yet you will plunge me in the ditch.
verses.32: For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him,
verses.33: There is no umpire between us,
verses.34: Let him take his rod away from me.
verses.35: then I would speak, and not fear him,
